title: argemone squarrosa
intro: Argemone squarrosa, also known as Mexican prickly poppy, is a plant species belonging to the poppy family (Papaveraceae) that is native to Mexico and parts of the southwestern United States. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its alkaloid content. The health benefits of Argemone squarrosa include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve wound healing, as well as its use as a natural remedy for fever reduction and cough relief. The plant is often used in herbal preparations such as tinctures, teas, and salves, which can be made by infusing the plant's seeds or leaves in a solvent like ethanol or oil.
